zh.lifeSummary: 當Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun於大約0987年5月2日出生在法國艾-吉洛易a奧度時，他的父親Signeur Warin de Montagne23歲，母親Melisende de Châteaudun20歲。他於大約1012年與Helvise de Corbon結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1039年12月15日在法國努維勒-阿吉泰洛吉吉加洛享年52歲，埋葬在法國阿奎泰洛吉吉加洛。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun于大约0987年5月2日出生在法国艾-吉洛易a奧度时，他的父亲Signeur Warin de Montagne23岁，母亲Melisende de Châteaudun20岁。他于大约1012年与Helvise de Corbon结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1039年12月15日在法国努維勒-阿吉泰洛吉吉加洛享年52岁，埋葬在法国阿奎泰洛吉吉加洛。

nameMeanings.name: du
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Perche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Deitrich, Gunter, Otto.
Danish: from Lærkia, a medieval nickname meaning ‘lark’.
Danish: habitational name from a place in Westphalia, Germany.

variants.name: Lerch
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Franz, Hans, Horst, Arno, Egon, Erwin, Gerhard, Guenter, Gunter, Helmut, Ingeborg, Karl-Heinz.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German lerche, German Lerche ‘lark’, probably a nickname for someone with a good singing voice, or a metonymic occupational name for a bird-catcher. As a Jewish surname, it is most probably artificial.

nameMeanings.name: Geoffroy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

nameMeanings.name: Ier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from Yiddish ber ‘bear’, probably influenced by the early medieval European practice of giving animal names to people. It is often paired with Dov in order to provide a Hebrew name in certain rituals.

en.lifeSummary: When Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun was born about 2 May 0987, in Châteaudun, Eure-et-Loir, France, his father, Signeur Warin de Montagne, was 23 and his mother, Melisende de Châteaudun, was 20. He married Helvise de Corbon about 1012. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died on 15 December 1039, in Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, at the age of 52, and was buried in Lot-et-Garonne, Aquitaine, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun est né environ 2 mai 0987, à Châteaudun, Eure-et-Loir, France, son père, Signeur Warin de Montagne, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Melisende de Châteaudun, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Helvise de Corbon environ 1012. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé le 15 décembre 1039, à Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterré à Lot-et-Garonne, Aquitaine, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun um 2. Mai 0987 in Châteaudun, Eure-et-Loir, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Signeur Warin de Montagne 23 und seine Mutter Melisende de Châteaudun 20. Er hat um 1012 Helvise de Corbon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb am 15. Dezember 1039 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Frankreich und wurde in Lot-et-Garonne, Aquitaine, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun è nato il circa 2 maggio 0987 nel Châteaudun, Eure-et-Loir, Francia, il suo padre, Signeur Warin de Montagne, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Melisende de Châteaudun, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Helvise de Corbon circa 1012. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì il 15 dicembre 1039, nel Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolto a Lot-et-Garonne, Aquitaine, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudunが約0987年5月2日にフランスエウレ-エト-ロイルチaテアウダンで生まれた時，父Signeur Warin de Montagneは23歳，母Melisende de Châteaudunは20歳であった。約1012年にHelvise de Corbonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1039年12月15日にフランスノウベレ-アクイティアインロト-エト-ガーオンで52歳で死去し，フランスアクイティアインロト-エト-ガーオンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun(이)가 대략 0987년 5월 2일 프랑스 우레-트-로이르 cha테오던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Signeur Warin de Montagne(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Melisende de Châteaudun(은)는 20세였다. 그는 대략 1012년 Helvise de Corbon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1039년 12월 15일 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 로트-트-가니에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 아키테인 로트-트-가니에 묻혔다.

pt.ABT 2 MAY 987–15 DEC 1039: aproximadamente 2 de maio de 0987–15 de dezembro de 1039
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun nasceu aproximadamente 2 de maio de 0987, em Châteaudun, Eure-et-Loir, França, seu pai, Signeur Warin de Montagne, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Melisende de Châteaudun, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Helvise de Corbon aproximadamente 1012. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele faleceu em 15 de dezembro de 1039, em Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, França, com 52 anos, e foi sepultado em Lot-et-Garonne, Aquitaine, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun родился около 2 мая 0987 г. в местности чaтодун, эуре-ет-лоир, Франция, его отцу по имени Signeur Warin de Montagne было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Melisende de Châteaudun, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Helvise de Corbon около 1012 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер 15 декабря 1039 г. в местности лот-ет-гаронн, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция в возрасте 52 лет и был похоронен в местности лот-ет-гаронн, аквитеин, Франция.

Se casó con Helvise de Corbon aproximadamente 1012. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió el 15 de diciembre de 1039, en Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultado en Lot-et-Garonne, Aquitaine, Francia.
